source_documents: https://www.sebi.gov.in (SEBI Prohibition of Insider Trading Regulations 2015 + enforcement orders)
category: Market abuse - trading on unpublished price-sensitive information
risk_signals: Well-timed trades by insiders or connected persons just before material announcements
Unusual volume or option activity ahead of price-sensitive news
Trading through relatives, associates, or entities to distance the insider
analyst_reasoning: Insider trading monetises unpublished price-sensitive information ahead of the market. The signature is timing and connection: trades that cluster just before announcements and originate from insiders or their proxies, which is what insider-trading regulation and surveillance are built to detect.
investigation_steps: Overlay trading activity against the timeline of material corporate announcements
Map connected persons, relatives, and controlled entities around the insider
Screen for abnormal volume, price, and derivatives activity pre-announcement
decision: Refer for market-surveillance investigation, restrict/monitor accounts, and pursue enforcement where a UPSI link is established.
outcome: Insider trading distorts market fairness; pattern-of-timing plus connection evidence underpins enforcement action.
lessons_learned: Timing relative to announcements plus insider connection is the core signal
Trading through relatives and associates is the standard concealment device
